description: Governance + enforcement ledger for every specification that steers Datum development. Enforced by scripts/check_spec_governance.py (wired into scripts/run_drift_gates.sh). Every .md under enforced_globs plus every tracked_doc MUST have an entry. Classes: 'governed' (tracked in PROGRESS and/or by a real gate), 'doctrine' (ratified decision record, controlling), 'pending' (known orphan with a remediation note), 'historical' (superseded/frozen, exempt with a reason). Every non-historical entry MUST carry an 'enforcement' block: enforced_by = VERIFIED references (check_NAME / ci:check_NAME / proof_gate:LABEL / spec_parity:INVENTORY / tests:PATH) that the gate confirms exist, and/or gap = an explicit statement of what is NOT enforced. See CLAUDE.md -> 'Specification Governance (controlling)'.

enforcement.gap: Native shard schema is prose-anchored; not fully field-level parity-locked.
Interactive GUI schematic editor is not built; spec is target-state.

The Section 4 per-standard disposition table is ungated prose; enforced checks cover only process-aperture + clearance/silk/peer-aperture/zone-fill repairs. Named IPC/IEEE numbers are not mechanically substantiated.
Proven by cargo tests only; no drift gate. The spec's target-vs-shipped divergence (10-variant vs shipped 6-variant PinElectricalType; ErcViolation vs ErcFinding) is not machine-reconciled.
Resolver/diagnostics proven by cargo tests only; no connectivity-owned drift gate. Byte-stable graph determinism only indirectly touched by the import determinism gate.
Only UUID/determinism + import-map identity are gated. KiCad/Eagle feature matrices, sidecar precedence, round-trip fidelity, and IPC-2581 are ungated prose. Import is frozen per North Star.

specs/M7_FRONTEND_SPEC.md.reason: M7 review-spike frontend spec; superseded by GUI decisions 014/015 and the North Star. Not an active implementation target (its own header still self-declares Active; the governed truth is historical).

enforcement.gap: Strongest-enforced contract (prose phrase-lock + 114-item pool_library_surface parity hash + PG tests), but the four-tool minimal-set semantics, LibraryGraph engine ownership, and IPC-7351B generation remain partial.
Two prose phrases + a private-writer ban on the 3 authoring files are locked; the ten-operation normalization, ComponentInstance mint/bind completeness, and revision-keyed ERC/waiver are ungated.
Standards check/repair surface parity-locked (owner is CHECKING_ARCHITECTURE_SPEC) + PG check-run/standards tests; the four-tool set, rules-source-shard migration, and revision-keyed ERC are ungated.

Mechanisms gated (datum.pcb.* count-lock, journaled ZoneFill, daemon write-fence); the 13-tool matrix, the commit() write-collapse thesis, align_distribute, and CLI undo/redo are ungated. The contract doc itself is not content-locked.
Journaled production/evidence writers + output-job/variant/panelization PG tests enforced; the three-surface lean export thesis, T0 single-projection unification, and panelization are ungated.

Runtime taxonomy hard-locked (public count/prefix + daemon write-parity fence + MCP_API parity); the DatumToolSession object, generic commit() coverage across all surfaces, and a unified Proposal primitive are not-yet. The contract doc itself is not content-locked.

GENUINE GAP: no drift gate enforces the layout invariants (child-in-parent, no sibling overlap, solver-backed completion) or the 1.0/1.25/1.5/2.0 visual-regression matrix. Enforcement is checked-in goldens + unit tests only. Highest-priority gap to close with a shell-grid contract gate.
GUI multi-view UX is only lightly covered.
Feasibility-analysis doc; the mechanism it argues feasible is backed by substrate tests, the doc itself is unchecked.
The typed-op/commit substrate the editor rides on is gated; the manual-editor GUI workflow (run ERC/DRC, navigate findings, direct manual editing) has no dedicated gate.
Project/workspace substrate is gated; workspace UX (view navigation across schematic/PCB/library/manufacturing, workspace restore) is not gated.
Standards repair/check slice enforced; the IPC-7351B structured-basis footprint generator is unbuilt (doctrine-only). IPC-7351B basis to be consolidated here from OPEN_QUESTION_RESOLUTIONS.

GENUINE GAP: no dedicated grid/layout-invariant gate; the decision's own 'shell grid contract test' is aspirational. Only incidental render_contract_tests.rs coverage (render-stack order, panel health).

docs/decisions/PRODUCT_MECHANICS_OPEN_QU.remediation: Ratified resolutions log (e.g. IPC-7351B basis) should be consolidated into numbered decision records (010). Do not add new ratifications here.

enforcement.gap: Unenforced log: individual resolutions are echoed by other decisions' gates (e.g. 010 standards PGs), but the log file itself is untracked and nothing checks development against it.

GENUINE GAP: the normative content (IPC-7351B generator, structured IpcFootprintBasis, density/toe-heel-side, governed mask/paste-in-export) is unbuilt and ungated. Existing footprint tests lock generic pool/import behavior, not this doc's IPC generator.

Underlying pool/library substrate is gated (library foundation contract + PG-LIBRARY-AUTHORING), but this per-user symbol-import normalization/provenance contract has no spec-specific test; skeleton import only today.

docs/STANDARDS_AUDIT_BATCH_1_GUIDANCE.md.remediation: Standards Audit Batch 1 guidance; referenced by the PROGRESS batch section. Govern, or mark historical once the batch closes.

enforcement.gap: Process/bridging guidance; its outcomes land in STANDARDS_COMPLIANCE_SPEC Section 4 dispositions, which are ungated. No enforceable code surface.

docs/STANDARDS_COMPLIANCE_INTEGRATION_GU.remediation: Research->spec integration meta-guidance; partially realized in STANDARDS_COMPLIANCE_SPEC. Fold into that spec or govern in the standards governance pass.

enforcement.gap: Process doc (research->docs/*GUIDANCE*->specs change-control rule); nothing enforces the change-control rule and it has no code surface.

docs/CHECKING_ARCHITECTURE.md.reason: Non-normative design rationale; superseded by the governed specs/CHECKING_ARCHITECTURE_SPEC.md (gated via check_spec_parity standards_check_surface). The doc self-declares non-normative.

enforcement.gap: PLAN's 'Active Execution Window' still centers on M7/imported-board fidelity though it is authority-demoted below the North Star; the wording should be reconciled.

Named controlling doctrine by README/CLAUDE, but no content gate reads it, and its header still self-declares 'draft foundation document for owner review' — inconsistent with its controlling role; reconcile the status line or add a marker gate.
Backlog of candidate decompositions; the aggregate budgets are gated, individual backlog items are not.

docs/PRODUCTION_CLEANUP_MANIFEST.md.remediation: Point-in-time worktree cleanup manifest (dated 2026-06-19); confirm whether still in use or retire as historical.

enforcement.gap: One-time cleanup manifest; no ongoing enforceable surface.
Text-engine module inventory hash-locked; behavioral text-fidelity completeness is partial.
Imported-text-normalization plan feeding the text engine; module inventory hash-locked; superseded in part by the Phase 2 text-engine plan.

Harness + checked-in multi-scale goldens are live infra, but the visual-regression matrix runs only under the visual feature / #[ignore]; no CI gate executes it yet (the PG-UI-LAYOUT-INVARIANTS gate is the intended closer).
Render-semantic vocabulary (stack order, copper colors, dim legibility, evidence markers) partly enforced by render_contract_tests; full semantic coverage not gated.
Render-stack layer discipline partly enforced by render_contract_tests stack-order assertions; not fully gated.
Copper material colors partly enforced by render_contract_tests; broader copper guidance not gated.
Airwire rendering partly covered by render tests; guidance not fully gated.
